pages.text: Mazi is sad. His older brother Boa, and big sister Rona, don’t want to play with him.
Boa finds Mazi crying. “What’s wrong Mazi?” Boa asks. “I want to play with you, but you don’t want to play with me,” cries Mazi.
“But nnoo ccrryyiinngg, okay?” Boa tells Mazi. Boa feels bad and allows Mazi “Okay,” to play dodgeball with them. Mazi says, excited.
Boa and Rona explain the dodgeball game to Mazi. He must stand in the middle and dodge the beachball while Boa and Rona try to hit him with it.
After they explain the game to Mazi, Rona throws the ball at him. a eM g d z”! i oshe shouts. D “ The ball hits him on the head.
h i c e u e! “ ” O“You hit me with the ball!” “But that’s the game,” says Rona, laughing. cries Mazi, as he throws himself onto the ground. “Okay. I will try again,” smiles Mazi.

Boa throws the ball at Mazi, who’s standing in the middle. Mazi catches the ball and runs with it around the tree. ac n t a h ’ Y o t uc c m “! ” e he shouts. “Mazi stop!” Boa and Rona shout at the same time.
Boa explains the rules to Mazi once more. Then they try again. This time, Rona goes in the middle and Boa throws the ball. Rona dodges the ball and it goes to Mazi’s side.
Mazi kicks the ball so high it gets stuck in the tree. “Maaziii!” they shout in frustration. “I’m sorry,” he says with his head down.
“You have to follow the rules of the game, Mazi, or else we won’t play with you,” Boa says. “Stand in the middle and let’s try again.”
Rona throws the ball, and this time Mazi dodges it. FINALLY!
